notes: This file is owned by Agent 08 only.
Structure nests rounds inside sprints to keep history organized.

sprint_meta.notes: WK68 Track G (UX): G0 diagnose + G1 fix buttons (H3 scroll/clip) DONE; G2 Hire-Hero button + H1 pause-guard fix DONE.

wk68_g0_diagnose.confirmed_root_cause: H3 (hit-rect coordinate space) -- NOT H1 pause-guard, NOT H2 masked exception.
diagnosis: The building panel renders ALL content into a tall scratch sub-surface (panel_surf, scratch_h up to 2400px), then blits only a CLIPPED, scroll-offset slice into the left-column viewport Rect(panel_x, panel_y, panel_width, viewport_h) with src_y=menu_scroll_px (building_panel.py:333-341).

But every interactive rect is stored at its UN-scrolled scratch position: enter_building_button_rect = Rect(panel_x+local.x, panel_y+local.y, ...) with NO menu_scroll_px term (building_panel.py:502-507); same for demolish (:461-466) and research/blacksmith/library/upgrade/build_catalog in the 3 building_renderers modules. menu_scroll_px appeared NOWHERE in rect computation.

Consequence: when panel content overflows the left-column viewport (hero pinned / 720p / 4-occupant guild), the bottom buttons (Enter renders last/lowest) fall BELOW the clip and are unclickable; scrolling does not help because the stored rect stays frozen at its scratch y while the drawn button moves with src_y.

Evidence lines: building_panel.py:502-507 (enter rect, no scroll term), :461-466 (demolish), :333-341 (clipped scroll-blit src_y=menu_scroll_px), :136-140 (outer bounds-gate). Same pattern in building_renderers/{economic,special,castle}_panel.py. Close (X) at top is unaffected (top-pinned).
ranked_hypotheses_ruled_out: H1 (input_handler.py:421-427 pause-guard) -- left in place; the click reaches building_panel.handle_click fine when the panel is visible. NOT the cause (separate latent issue; Agent 11 owns the paused-click regression test per PM brief).
H2 (try/except at input_handler.py:445) -- no exception fires in the click ladder; the click is simply rejected by the frozen-rect/viewport mismatch. NOT the cause.
wk68_g0_diagnose.reported_to_pm: Confirmed cause H3 + exact lines reported; PM APPROVED root cause and the option-1 + viewport-clamp fix for G1.

what_i_changed: game/ui/building_panel.py: NEW _rect_in_viewport(rect) helper -- True iff a stored interactive rect.center is inside the visible clipped viewport Rect(panel_x, panel_y, panel_width, panel_height). Gates every interactive collidepoint so a button scrolled above/below the clip cannot be clicked through.

game/ui/building_panel.py handle_click: wrapped every interactive collidepoint with _rect_in_viewport(...) -- enter, demolish, research(marketplace), build_catalog(castle), upgrade(palace), and the library/blacksmith research loops. Close (X) left as-is (top-pinned). Preserved exact action dicts: {type:enter_building}, {type:demolish_building}, {type:open_build_catalog}, truthy fallbacks.

game/ui/building_panel.py update_hover: same _rect_in_viewport gate on demolish/enter/build_catalog/upgrade/research hover + the library/blacksmith hover loops so hover highlight only registers on visible rects.

game/ui/building_panel.py _render_demolish_button (:461) and _render_enter_building_button (:502): store rect y as panel_y + local_rect.y - self.menu_scroll_px so the screen rect tracks the on-screen (scrolled, clipped) button position.

game/ui/building_panel.py render(): pre-clamp self.menu_scroll_px against the prior frame _menu_max_scroll BEFORE rendering content, so the rect stores use the SAME scroll offset as the final blit src_y (the end-of-frame clamp only tightens if content shrank).
game/ui/building_renderers/economic_panel.py: research_button_rect (:106) and blacksmith_research_rects (:239) now subtract panel.menu_scroll_px from the stored y.
game/ui/building_renderers/special_panel.py: library_research_rects (:92) and palace upgrade_button_rect (:210) now subtract panel.menu_scroll_px from the stored y.
game/ui/building_renderers/castle_panel.py: build_catalog_button_rect (:72) now subtracts panel.menu_scroll_px from the stored y.
scope_constraints_respected: Functional fix ONLY: no typed action enum, no dispatch-ladder restructure (Round B).
Stayed in lane: game/ui/building_panel.py + building_renderers/{economic,special,castle}_panel.py. Did NOT touch input_handler.py pause-guard (H1 is a separate latent issue; flagged for Agent 11).
Preserved exact action dicts/strings the input_handler consumers expect (input_handler.py:623-643).
verification: Throwaway repro (overflow case: 1280x720, short 300x240 left_rect, 4-occupant warrior_guild): at scroll=0 enter_rect center (150,648) is BELOW the 240px viewport -> _rect_in_viewport False, handle_click returns False (no phantom enter). After scroll-to-bottom (menu_scroll_px=425) enter_rect center (150,223) is INSIDE viewport -> _rect_in_viewport True, handle_click(center) returns {type:enter_building, building:<WarriorGuild>}. Back-to-top -> off-viewport again, handle_click False. REPRO PASS.

Overflow screenshots with clickable-rect outlines: scroll0 shows Demolish/Enter rects red (off-viewport, correctly rejected); scrolled shows the magenta/cyan rect outlines pixel-overlapping the drawn Demolish/Enter buttons inside the viewport (clickable).

commands_run.note: 603 passed, 4 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 601; +2 are Agent 04 wk68 determinism tests, none of mine regressed)
7 PNGs; non-overflow case (inn panel, Demolish+Enter visible+clickable)
7 PNGs; inn panel still fits at 720p (0 occupants -> short content, no overflow)

evidence.visual_verdict: PASS. Alignment/layering checked first: panel left edge flush x=0, buttons left-aligned x=10 with consistent margins, Demolish above Enter, no overlap with world. The stored clickable rect tracks the scrolled on-screen button position exactly (rect outline overlaps drawn button) and the viewport gate blocks off-screen clicks. Both the non-overflow case (buttons visible+clickable) and the overflow case (buttons reachable after scroll) verified.

wk68_g1_fix_buttons.notes: ui_panels scenario selects an inn with 0 occupants so it never overflows the left column -- as anticipated in the PM brief, I captured the overflow case via a dedicated warrior_guild render with a short (hero-pinned/720p-style) left_rect. G2 (Hire-Hero button) is still PENDING PM DISPATCH -- not started in this wave. DID NOT COMMIT.

PART_A_hire_button: game/ui/building_panel.py: added module constant _HIRABLE_TYPES=frozenset({warrior_guild,ranger_guild,rogue_guild,wizard_guild,temple}) (comment: source of truth engine.py:962 allowed); imported HERO_HIRE_COST from config.

game/ui/building_panel.py: added self.hire_hero_button_rect + self.hire_hero_button_hovered next to enter_building_button_rect (~ln 58-59); reset hire_hero_button_rect=None each frame in render() next to the other rect resets (~ln 297).

game/ui/building_panel.py: new _render_hire_hero_button(self, surface, building, y, economy) -- gated on _HIRABLE_TYPES + is_constructed; enabled = can_hire AND player_gold>=HERO_HIRE_COST; draws blue button "Hire Hero  00" with greyed/disabled state (bg_disabled + text_disabled_color) + sub-line "Heroes: hired/max"; stores hit-rect ONLY when enabled using the G1 scroll-aware convention Rect(panel_x+local.x, panel_y+local.y - menu_scroll_px, w, h); called right after _render_enter_building_button in render() (~ln 326).

game/ui/building_panel.py: handle_click -- new branch before demolish (gated by self.hire_hero_button_rect and _rect_in_viewport + collidepoint) returns {"type":"hire_hero","building":building} when constructed + can_hire.
game/ui/building_panel.py: update_hover -- new hire_hero_button_hovered entry gated with _rect_in_viewport like the others.

game/input_handler.py: new consumer in the building-panel result block after the enter_building branch (~ln 642) -- on {"type":"hire_hero"} sets c.selected_building=building then calls no-arg c.try_hire_hero(); returns. try_hire_hero() kept NO-ARG (H-hotkey at :322 + command-bar caller untouched). engine.py NOT modified.
PART_B_h1_pause_guard: game/input_handler.py: H1 fix at the paused-non-modal guard (~ln 420-440). Previously dropped EVERY panel click when c.paused and no pause_menu/memorial/interior modal visible. Now also computes panel_hit = (event.button==1 and building_panel.visible and Rect(panel_x,panel_y,panel_width,panel_height).collidepoint(event.pos)); the early-return now fires only if event.button!=1 OR not (mem_vis or bio_vis or panel_hit). World-selection clicks stay blocked because building_panel.handle_click returns True (consumes) for any click inside the panel bounds before world pick is reached -- only panel-area clicks pass through.

scope_constraints_respected: Functional only -- no typed HudAction/dispatch refactor; mirrored the Enter/Demolish button idiom.
Lane respected: edited game/ui/building_panel.py + game/input_handler.py only. engine.py untouched (no guild= param needed -- setting selected_building auto-targets try_hire_hero).
Preserved all existing action strings + the no-arg try_hire_hero() hotkey/command-bar callers.

verification.pytest: python -m pytest -q -> 609 passed, 4 skipped, 0 failed, 1 warning (131s). No regressions; Agent 11 to add formal hire+paused tests next.
verification.functional_repro_hire: Throwaway repro: clicking the hire hit-rect routed through InputHandler.handle_mousedown -> gold 500->400 (-100), guild.heroes_hired 0->1, len(engine.heroes) 0->1, new hero.home_building==warrior_guild, and selected_building re-targeted to the clicked guild even though a DIFFERENT building was engine.selected_building before the click.
verification.functional_repro_disabled: Broke (gold=50) -> hire_hero_button_rect is None (not clickable). At cap (heroes_hired==max_heroes) -> hire_hero_button_rect is None.
verification.functional_repro_h1_paused: With engine.paused=True + no pause_menu/memorial/interior modal, a click on the hire button is now PROCESSED (gold-100, heroes_hired+1). Control: a paused world-click OUTSIDE the panel (1000,600) is STILL blocked (selected_building unchanged, no hero spawned) -- guard not over-widened.

evidence.visual_verdict: PASS. Alignment/layering FIRST: the Hire Hero button is left-flush and same width as Demolish + Enter Building, sits directly below Enter Building, no overlap, no clipping, within the scroll viewport. ENABLED: blue button "Hire Hero  00" + greyish sub-line "Heroes: 0/8" legible on all 5 hirable types (incl. Temple, correctly gated). DISABLED: broke + at-cap both render the button greyed (dark-grey fill, dimmed text) with a greyed sub-line and store NO hit-rect; at-cap panel header also shows "Heroes: 8/8" in red.

H1 was a latent issue (Agent 11 owns the formal paused-click test). DO NOT COMMIT per PM gate.

wk133_quests_vertical_slice.task: WK126-T9 (Wave 2): quest-create dialog (type -> target -> reward tier) opened from a selected Herald's Post + active-quest readout (quest_view_panel) + tests + headless screenshots.
what_i_changed: game/ui/quest_create_panel.py (NEW): QuestCreatePanel centered modal modeled on BuildCatalogPanel (same panel_modal/button 9-slice chrome, header + X, click-outside cancels). Single-screen 3-step flow: (1) 4 quest-type pill buttons (raid_lair/slay_enemy_type/find_poi/explore_far); (2) target picker per type - raid: DISCOVERED lairs listed name+distance-in-tiles (is_lair, hp>0, center tile Visibility>=SEEN; missing fog reads as discovered so captures/tests never blank), slay: 5 huntable enemy types (goblin/wolf/skeleton/spider/bandit) + count stepper default QUEST_SLAY_DEFAULT_COUNT=5 clamp 1..25, find_poi: discovered non-depleted sim.pois (poi_def.display_name + distance), explore: 4 compass presets toward far map edges (8% margin tiles, charted/unexplored tag); (3) reward tiers Low/Med/High = QUEST_REWARD_LOW/MED/HIGH ($60/$140/$280) with cost shown, unaffordable tiers disabled + build-menu-style 'Need $X (have $Y)' feedback. Confirm calls THE Wave-1 engine action sim.create_quest(post.entity_id, type, target, reward, count) (escrow via economy.fund_quest; None -> feedback, modal stays open). Modal embeds the active-quest board (QuestViewPanel.render_active_quests). Sizes derived from surface (panel clamps to short windows); screen-space hit-rects; no RNG; sim mutation only via the explicit player Confirm.

game/ui/quest_view_panel.py: extended the wk14 ViewMode.QUEST placeholder with render_active_quests(surface, rect, game_state) -> list[str]: 'Active Quests' board, 2 lines per quest ('<Type> - <target>' / '$<reward> - Open|Accepted: <hero> (n/m)'), pixel-true truncation for narrow columns, '+N more' overflow line, 'No active quests.' empty state; returns rendered lines for headless assertions. quest_target_label() shared helper (slay '5x goblin', explore 'tile (x,y)', raid/poi display name). render() now shows the board in the old travelogue placeholder area and under the hero-None label.

game/ui/building_panel.py (the selection-wiring file): BuildingPanel owns self.quest_create_panel; herald_post card renders a 'Create Quest' button (royal-blue, hire-button pattern incl. G1 scroll-aware hit-rect + viewport gate; greyed no-hit-rect while under construction) with 'Fund a quest for heroes to take' sub-line; handle_click opens the modal with game_state (game_state['sim'] -> SimEngine); while the modal is open handle_click forwards EVERY click to it FIRST (before the panel bounds check) and always consumes - mouse.py reaches building_panel.handle_click for all clicks while a building is selected, so world clicks cannot leak through the modal (same semantics as the build catalog; click outside the modal cancels); update_hover forwards; deselect() closes a dangling modal; modal renders LAST in BuildingPanel.render (building_panel renders after HUD in render_coordinator -> modal on top, under pause menu).
game/input/keyboard.py: ESC closes the quest-create modal first (new top check in the esc branch, before chat/interior/pause-menu - modal gets ESC priority).

tools/screenshot_scenarios.py: NEW scenario wk133_quest_ui (registered in get_scenario): constructed herald_post + real GoblinCamp lair + hero, revealed world; 3 shots - selected-post card w/ Create Quest button, modal mid-flow (raid type + lair target + Med selected), modal w/ a funded slay quest on the Active Quests board (top-bar gold visibly debited 500->360).

ESC needed the one allowed game/input/keyboard.py touch.
findings: The wk14 ViewMode.QUEST right-column render path is DORMANT: MicroViewManager.render has no callers since the WK130 sidebar overhaul removed the right column (hud.py render sets _show_right_panel = False). The live, player-reachable surface for the active-quest board is therefore the quest-create modal (which embeds QuestViewPanel.render_active_quests); the QUEST-mode render was also extended so it is correct if ever re-wired.

The tan squares visible in empty modal areas are the shared panel_modal.png 9-slice pattern (also present in ui_build_catalog captures), not a new artifact.

wk135_inventory_ui.task: Sovereign feedback: WK131 items render only as text in the hero panel; no discoverable inventory UI. Built a real inventory window (equipment slot boxes + backpack grid + potions row, rarity-colored) opened via hero-panel Inventory button, watch-card Bag button, and hotkey I; documented before/after with the exact 1-hero/5-item repro scenario.
what_i_changed: game/ui/inventory_panel.py (NEW): InventoryPanel centered modal modeled on quest_create_panel.py (the newer template: ModalPanel backdrop + 9-slice header chrome + close X + click-outside/ESC dismissal; chosen over build_catalog_panel which lacks the header/X pattern). Equipment section: 3 labeled 52px slot boxes (Weapon/Armor/Accessory) with item-initials marker, item name + key stats beside each ('Steel Sword / +10 ATK'), rarity-colored border+name (common gray-white / uncommon green / rare blue / legendary orange) resolved via game/content/items.py (ItemDef.rarity; legacy equip dicts via 'id' -> ITEMS, then find_by_name, fallback common). Empty slots dimmed ('Empty' box + 'None'). Backpack section: header 'Backpack n/cap' + exactly backpack_capacity slot boxes, filled = rarity border + name under the box, empty = dimmed em-dash boxes. Consumables row: drawn potion-flask marker + 'Potions n/max'. Footer hint: 'Heroes auto-equip upgrades and sell spare loot at shops.' + 'View only - heroes manage their own gear. I / ESC closes.' (indirect-control design: VIEW, not drag-drop). Panel 560x412, clamps to short windows (never hardcodes 1080p), on_resize clears the backdrop cache. Pure visual, no sim writes, no RNG.

game/ui/hero_panel.py: 'Inventory (I)' button (amber) directly below the Chat button - exact Chat pattern (raw rect + viewport-visibility flag + hover + screen-space hit rect); handle_click returns {'type':'open_inventory','hero':...}. Plus a right-aligned '(I) Inventory' affordance hint on the Gear section header line.

game/ui/hud.py: owns the panel (hud.inventory_panel), renders it last in HUD.render (topmost, with dead/deselected-hero auto-close guard), on_resize forwards, handle_click consumes ALL clicks while the modal is open (returns 'inventory_click'); watch-card Bag-button hit branch returns open_inventory for the pinned hero.

game/ui/hud_watch_card.py: small 'Bag' button next to the Chat button in the stats-row slack, drawn ONLY when it fits (cw=246: Chat ends ~x+195, Bag ~34px ends ~x+233 <= x+240) - card height + WATCH_CARD_* constants unchanged; _inventory_open_rect reset per frame in render_card_slot.

game/input/keyboard.py: ESC closes the inventory window first (before the quest modal); hotkey 'i' toggles it for the selected hero (guarded hasattr(hero,'backpack') so Guards/TaxCollector are no-ops). 'i' verified free: BUILD_HOTKEY_TO_TYPE = 1-9/t/u (WK114 deleted the old library 'I' hotkey).

game/input/mouse.py (small lane extension, reported): 2 routing branches - 'inventory_click' consumed (otherwise the click would fall through to world selection and clear the hero), and open_inventory dict -> c.hud.inventory_panel.open(hero). Mirrors the existing chat_band_open/start_conversation routing.

tools/screenshot_scenarios.py: scenario_wk135_inventory_ui - ONE warrior in an empty world (dynamic entities cleared in apply too, since capture ticks respawn enemies; show_perf off) with EXACTLY 5 items (equip steel_sword/chain_mail/hawk_signet via hero.equip + dagger/long_bow via add_to_backpack; potions=1 counter state for the consumables row). Shots: before hero panel + before pinned watch card, after inventory window at 1920x1080 AND 1024x576, hero panel with button, watch card with Bag. NOTE: 1024x576 shot kept LAST - capture_screenshots.py only reconfigures the surface when a shot size differs from the CLI size, so a non-CLI-size shot leaks its surface into later default-size shots (pre-existing runner quirk, worked around in-scenario).

tests/test_wk135_inventory_ui.py (NEW, 16 tests): rarity mapping (ItemDef + legacy dict id/name fallbacks), window renders 3 equip names+stats / Backpack 2/5 with 2 filled + 3 empty / Potions 1/5, empty hero (Empty x3, None x3, 0/5, 5 empty boxes), X/outside/ESC close, hotkey I toggle + free-of-build-hotkeys + no-hero no-op, hero-panel button hit (below Chat, Chat unchanged), HUD modal click consumption, watch-card Bag button render-in-card + WATCH_CARD_* constants pinned, bounds at 1024x576 and 1920x1080. pygame.font.Font is an immutable C type -> spy via _SpyFont wrapper on the panel's font attrs, not monkeypatching Font.render.
wk135_inventory_ui.wiring_point: HUD owns the modal (like building_panel owns quest_create_panel): hud.render tail renders it topmost; hud.handle_click top consumes while visible; mouse.py routes 'inventory_click'/open_inventory; keyboard.py ESC-first close + 'i' toggle.
findings: Hotkey I is free (WK114 Round B removed the zombie building hotkeys G/E/V/Y/O/F/I/R); pinned by a test against BUILD_HOTKEY_TO_TYPE.

Watch-card decision: Bag button FITS in the existing stats-row slack next to Chat without growing the card - shipped (test asserts it stays inside the card rect and the WATCH_CARD_* constants are unchanged).

capture_screenshots.py size-switch quirk (compares shot size to CLI size, not current surface) documented in the scenario; the first re-capture run also OVERWROTE the original before PNGs - true before-state evidence preserved as wk135_BEFORE_hero_panel_crop.png / wk135_BEFORE_watch_card_crop.png (the wk135_before_*.png files regenerate with the new button present).

evidence.visual_verdict: Window: equipment boxes aligned in 3 columns with rarity borders (Steel Sword/Chain Mail green-uncommon, Hawk Signet blue-rare), Backpack 2/5 with Dagger(white-common)/Long Bow(green) + 3 empty boxes, Potions 1/5 with red flask, footer hints readable; fits centered and unclipped at both 1024x576 and 1920x1080. Hero panel: Chat + Inventory (I) buttons stacked, flush left edges, '(I) Inventory' hint right-aligned on the Gear header. Watch card: Chat|Bag side-by-side inside the card, no growth.

wk135_quickfix_stray_tan_squares.task: PM screenshot review: stray tan/beige placeholder squares floating inside BOTH the WK135 inventory window and the WK133 quest-create dialog. Find the shared source and remove the artifact; re-capture and verify.
wk135_quickfix_stray_tan_squares.root_cause: NOT panel draw code and NOT a theme/helper rect fill. The shared modal texture assets/ui/kingdomsim_ui_cc0/panels/panel_modal.png (32x32) contains a 4x4 grid of single-pixel light-tan 'rivet/stud' decoration pixels (160,140,100) on the (130,108,78) interior fill. NineSlice.render (game/ui/widgets.py:299) scales the 16x16 center source region up to the full modal body (e.g. 544x604), so each 1px stud is stretched into a ~34px flat tan square - the 2x2 grid of center studs (src 12/18) became the floating squares; both modals use the same texture via ModalPanel(texture_path=panel_modal.png), which is why the artifact appeared identically in both. The studs were designed for pixel-crisp 32x32 use (asset README) and were never visible as intended under 9-slice center scaling.
wk135_quickfix_stray_tan_squares.fix: Edited the texture at the true source: replaced all 16 stud pixels (160,140,100,255) in panel_modal.png with the interior fill color (130,108,78,255) via PIL. Zero code changes - game/ui/inventory_panel.py and game/ui/quest_create_panel.py draw no such squares (verified by full read); fixing the texture also cleans the other panel_modal consumers (build catalog, pause menu, building panel) consistently. panel_top.png/panel_bottom.png have the same studs (visible as small tan rects in the long-standing HUD top/bottom bars) but were left untouched - pre-existing approved chrome, outside this round's flagged scope.

screenshot_verdict.pixel_check: exact-color scan: 0 stud-color pixels in any modal BODY; remaining (160,140,100) matches are confined to the header/button strips where button_normal.png legitimately uses that color as its top-light edge (intended button chrome).

sprint_meta.notes: WK138 adventure ledger foundation: make active multi-phase quest chains readable in the existing HUD/quest board, with a compact quest-create modal fallback for smaller viewports.

r1_kickoff.task: Make active multi-phase quests readable in the existing HUD/quest board.
what_i_changed: game/ui/quest_view_panel.py: added active quest-chain rendering to the existing board. It now pulls quest_chain snapshots from the sim/read-model, resolves assigned hero/reward/current phase, and renders chain status plus completed/current/upcoming phase labels. Full-size layouts show the chain card with the current objective; compact layouts now compress into a smaller title + summary/objective/timeline stack so the 1024x576 create modal stays readable without overlap.

tests/test_wk138_quest_chain_ui.py: NEW deterministic pygame fixture coverage for the active chain board, completed/failed outcomes, and compact small-row rendering. The tests also save proof PNGs under docs/screenshots/wk138_quest_chain_ui_panels.
docs/screenshots/wk138_quest_chain_ui_panels/: regenerated proof PNGs for the chain board and quest-create modal after each layout iteration.
